scenarios.title: Web client sends an authenticated turn and renders the response
scenarios.gherkin:
Given the web client has a valid same-origin HttpOnly session cookie established by the spec 070 web login flow with the assistant.turn scope
When the user types a NL message and submits it
Then the client POSTs to /api/assistant/turn with credentials: "same-origin" and a fresh transport_message_id
And the response body is rendered: text, citations, and any disambig/confirm/capture controls
scenarios.liveSystemExpectation: web Go-driven e2e-api row (TP-073-09) drives the served PWA assistant route and the live assistant HTTP endpoint, asserting schema-rendered body/sources/controls

scenarios.title: Shared mobile client uses generated types from the golden schema
scenarios.gherkin:
Given the shared mobile client is built for iPhone/iOS and Android with types generated from the spec 069 golden schema
When the wire schema is changed in a way that breaks compatibility
Then the shared mobile build fails at codegen before either mobile platform ships
And no incompatible iPhone/iOS or Android client ships to users
scenarios.liveSystemExpectation: build/unit codegen and renderer canaries guard web plus shared mobile schema drift before runtime; TP-073-03 asserts each surface emits a render-descriptor-v1 conforming descriptor deep-equal to the per-fixture golden under tests/fixtures/assistant_response_v1/

scenarios.title: Transient network failure retries with the same transport_message_id (web)
scenarios.gherkin:
Given the web client POSTs a turn and the request times out
When the user retries
Then the retry uses the SAME transport_message_id
And the server returns the same response (deduped)
scenarios.liveSystemExpectation: web Go-driven e2e-api retry row asserts the live server observes the same transport_message_id and returns the deduped response; adversarial sibling proves the parity check is not tautological

scenarios.title: Disambiguation prompt renders and round-trips on web and mobile
scenarios.gherkin:
Given a turn returns an AssistantResponse with a disambiguation prompt
When the user picks choice 2 on web, iPhone/iOS, and Android in separate sessions
Then every client POSTs kind = "disambiguation" with the same disambiguation_ref shape
And the eventual response on every client matches the chosen scenario's invocation result
scenarios.liveSystemExpectation: Deferred to follow-on spec; web+native mobile parity e2e-ui rows require iPhone/iOS and Android adapters

scenarios.title: Confirm card renders identically and round-trips
scenarios.gherkin:
Given a turn returns an AssistantResponse with a confirm card
When the user accepts the action
Then the side-effect-bearing path executes server-side
And web, iPhone/iOS, and Android render the post-confirm result with identical structure
scenarios.liveSystemExpectation: Deferred to follow-on spec; cross-surface confirm parity needs native mobile adapters

scenarios.title: Capture-as-fallback acknowledgement is identical to other transports
scenarios.gherkin:
Given the server returns AssistantResponse with CaptureRoute = true
When the client renders the response
Then the "saved-as-idea" acknowledgement appears with the same shape and copy as Telegram, HTTP-test, and WhatsApp
And no client-side capture logic exists; the server's response alone drives the UI
scenarios.liveSystemExpectation: Deferred to follow-on spec; canonical capture-ack shape is owned by spec 074 and consumed here once native adapters land

scenarios.title: No client-side scenario logic exists
scenarios.gherkin:
Given the response shape does not include a recognized control variant
When the client renders it
Then the client falls back to rendering the text body
And the client does NOT branch on scenario id, action class, or transport_hint to decide affordances
scenarios.liveSystemExpectation: Deferred to follow-on spec; cross-surface no-branch guard test is deferred (web JS renderer satisfies invariant by construction)

scenarios.title: Closed-vocabulary transport_hint is honored
scenarios.gherkin:
Given the web client sends transport_hint = "web" and the shared mobile client sends transport_hint = "mobile" on iPhone/iOS and Android
When the server processes all three clients
Then the server-side closed-vocabulary check accepts both values
And neither value alters scenario selection or response shape
scenarios.liveSystemExpectation: integration and e2e-api rows assert transport hints accepted and telemetry-only; live runs queued behind suite lock per report.md Known Drift

scenarios.title: Web client meets accessibility floor
scenarios.gherkin:
Given a screen reader is active on the web client
When the user submits a turn and the response arrives
Then the response area announces via an ARIA live region
And keyboard navigation reaches the composer, send button, disambig choices, and confirm buttons in a sensible order
scenarios.liveSystemExpectation: web Go-driven e2e-api row asserts aria-live response region, labelled composer, deterministic tab order; driver-based screen-reader announcement validation deferred to a future browser-driver foundation spec

scenarios.title: Shared mobile client meets VoiceOver and TalkBack accessibility floor
scenarios.gherkin:
Given VoiceOver is enabled on iPhone/iOS and TalkBack is enabled on Android
When the user submits a turn and the response arrives
Then the response renders with semantic labels readable by both mobile assistive technologies
And interactive controls (disambig list, confirm buttons) are focusable and announce their purpose on both mobile platforms
scenarios.liveSystemExpectation: Deferred to follow-on spec; on-device VoiceOver/TalkBack runs require native iPhone/iOS + Android adapters

scenarios.title: Missing backend base URL fails loud at build/start time
scenarios.gherkin:
Given the SST-derived backend base URL is unset for a client build
When the client is built or initialized
Then the build or initialization fails loud with a NO-DEFAULTS error naming the missing key
scenarios.liveSystemExpectation: unit/build rows prove web, iPhone/iOS, and Android use no backend URL fallback

scenarios.title: Shared mobile client uses generated types from the golden schema (Scope 3 duplicate)
scenarios.gherkin: Same as primary SCN-073-A02 entry; this duplicate records Scope 3 ownership for the deferred shared-mobile build target.
scenarios.liveSystemExpectation: Deferred to follow-on spec; native iPhone/iOS + Android platform-parity build canary requires the deferred adapters

scenarios.title: Transient network failure retries with the same transport_message_id (mobile)
scenarios.gherkin:
Given the shared mobile client POSTs a turn from iPhone/iOS and Android and the request times out
When the user retries on each platform
Then each retry uses the SAME transport_message_id for that platform's original turn
And the server returns the same response (deduped)
scenarios.liveSystemExpectation: Deferred to follow-on spec; mobile retry parity e2e-ui requires native adapters

scenarios.title: Missing backend base URL fails loud at build/start time (mobile duplicate)
scenarios.gherkin:
Given the SST-derived mobile backend base URL is unset for a shared mobile build
When the mobile client is built or initialized for iPhone/iOS or Android
Then the build or initialization fails loud with a NO-DEFAULTS error naming the missing key
scenarios.liveSystemExpectation: Deferred to follow-on spec; fail-loud surface on native mobile build requires the deferred adapters

scenarios.title: Closed-vocabulary transport_hint is honored (Scope 4 duplicate)
scenarios.gherkin: Same as primary SCN-073-A08; covered by SCOPE-073-01 TP-073-05 / TP-073-08 today.
scenarios.liveSystemExpectation: Deferred to follow-on spec for the cross-surface variant; web-only transport_hint coverage is live under SCN-073-A08 primary entry

scenarios.title: Browse topics index to a topic page
scenarios.gherkin:
Given the user opens the wiki surface
When the user selects "Topics"
Then the topics index lists topics from the knowledge graph with counts of linked artifacts, people, and places
And selecting a topic opens a topic page showing the topic's linked artifacts, related people, and related places
scenarios.liveSystemExpectation: Served /wiki/topics index and topic detail page assert against the live knowledge graph API; rendered HTML matches the schema fixture per route

scenarios.title: Browse people index to a person page
scenarios.gherkin:
Given the user opens the wiki surface
When the user selects "People"
Then the people index lists people derived from the intelligence layer with artifact counts
And selecting a person opens a person page showing a timeline of artifacts, related topics, and related places for that person
scenarios.liveSystemExpectation: Served /wiki/people index and person detail page assert against the live intelligence layer (internal/intelligence/people*.go) plus graph edges

scenarios.title: Browse places index to a place page
scenarios.gherkin:
Given the user opens the wiki surface
When the user selects "Places"
Then the places index lists places derived from the maps connector and any artifact-derived locations
And selecting a place opens a place page showing the place's map-derived location and linked artifacts
scenarios.liveSystemExpectation: Served /wiki/places index and place detail page assert against the maps-connector-derived places API plus graph edges

scenarios.title: Time view renders a calendar-style scroll
scenarios.gherkin:
Given the user opens the wiki surface
When the user selects "Time"
Then the time view renders artifacts grouped by day in a vertical calendar-style scroll
And the user can scroll backward and forward in time without losing scroll position when navigating away and back
scenarios.liveSystemExpectation: Served /wiki/time renders day-grouped artifacts from the live artifacts API; scroll-position preservation asserted via rendered DOM markers across navigation

scenarios.title: Cross-links render on every artifact page with explainable reasons
scenarios.gherkin:
Given the user is on any artifact, topic, person, or place page
When the page renders
Then a "Related" section lists graph-derived cross-links to other artifacts, topics, people, or places
And each cross-link carries an explainable reason sourced from the graph edge metadata
scenarios.liveSystemExpectation: Served detail pages render Related sections whose anchors and reason strings deep-equal the live graph edge response; adversarial sibling proves the assertion fails if the client re-derives or re-orders

scenarios.title: Inline annotation entry point opens from any artifact page
scenarios.gherkin:
Given the user is on an artifact page
And the spec 027 SCOPE-9 annotation endpoints (SCN-027-71..74) are available
When the user activates the "Annotate" entry point
Then an inline annotation editor opens scoped to the current artifact
And submitting calls the spec 027 SCOPE-9 annotation endpoints
And the rendered artifact page reflects the new annotation after submit
scenarios.liveSystemExpectation: When spec 027 SCOPE-9 endpoints are reachable, Annotate opens the inline editor and submit hits SCN-027-71..74; when unreachable, button renders aria-disabled with affordance; storage guard asserts no bearer/session material persists from wiki pages
